disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Violaceous flat-topped papules with fine white (Wickham) striae on flexor wrists/ankles; no finger web-space burrows; no itchy contacts
trigger_question_examples: Are the bumps flat-topped and purple with fine white lines on top?
Do you see any thin lines/burrows between your fingers, and is anyone else itchy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, flat-topped, purple, with fine white lines; no lines between my fingers, and no one else is itchy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Scratched areas becoming hot, spreading red, or draining pus (secondary infection)
trigger_question_examples: Are any scratched areas hot, spreading red, or oozing pus?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No pus or spreading redness, and no fever — just raw from scratching.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new drugs, soaps or exposures; no oral or genital lesions
trigger_question_examples: Any new medications, soaps or detergents recently?
Any sores in the mouth or genital area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No new products or medications, and nothing in the mouth or genitals.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this scabies? Do I need to treat my whole house?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that the violaceous flat-topped papules with Wickham striae and no burrows/contacts indicate an autoimmune skin condition, a non-contagious autoimmune rash, so household scabies treatment is not needed; arrange in-person confirmation and give itch relief with a secondary-infection safety-net.
